name: Democrats.org
subject: Send a message to Senate Republicans, Derek
body:
Let's get to work.
---
**Senate Republicans are negotiating in secret to repeal health care. Tell them to show us the bill now.

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Show-Us-The-Bill1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170616_joc_nd&source=em_20170616_joc_nd")**
Derek --

We're starting to see a recurring theme with Republicans in Congress: secrecy.

House Republicans wrote their health care repeal bill in secret. Now, Senate Republicans are doing the same thing.

Why? Because they know that repealing the ACA will hit the most vulnerable Americans -- including some of their own constituents -- the hardest.

Kicking people off their coverage, slashing Medicaid, undermining protections for people with pre-existing conditions -- none of that is what any of us signed up for. So, together with your help, we're going to shine a big, bright light on the GOP's health care repeal.

**Join more than 100,000 Democrats who have already signed on, and tell Senate Republicans we won't let them get away with secret health care repeal. Tell them to show us their bill -- now:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Show-Us-The-Bill1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170616_joc_nd&source=em_20170616_joc_nd")**
Thanks,

Jess

| | | | Peter,

I can't tell you how excited I am to invite you to the 11th annual [Defending the American Dream Summit](http://click.myprosperitymail.com/?qs=40b98822729e4198f358e899db55cf4ff2b75edb76fc8ccb92862d90a563054eb2099136e01ed75da50823039e0063c12fb0b0f70104102d "Defending the American Dream Summit") in Richmond, Va., on Saturday, August 19th!
Each year the Defending the American Dream Summit brings together top activists and volunteers from around the country in a one-of-a-kind celebration of our grassroots movement.

name: Senator Maggie Hassan
subject: my grandmother would be disappointed
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello everyone,
My grandmother used to say that if you have your health, you have everything.
Unfortunately, the Trumpcare bill being written in secret by Republican men is sure to put access to affordable health care in jeopardy for millions of Americans.
That's why I called on Senate Republicans to hold hearings and share details with the public about the bill they've been working on behind closed doors.
[**Lives are literally on the line here, so please add your name to join me and tell Mitch McConnell and Senate Republicans not to bring Trumpcare to a vote without holding hearings, consulting experts, or letting us bring the bill back home to share with you.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde82/2a380d31/1b47d7e2/fe9657/1356227807/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20170616_protectaffcare?source=e20170616_healthsignon_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
This bill is closer than ever to becoming law, and we hear that it's just as bad as the horrendous House bill. In fact, Mitch McConnell wants to bring it to a vote before the end of the month.
[**Add your name to call on Republicans in the Senate not to force their dangerous health care plan to a vote.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde82/2a380d31/1b47d7e2/fe9657/1356227807/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20170616_protectaffcare?source=e20170616_healthsignon_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
If this bill is what we expect, it will gut funding for Planned Parenthood, allow insurance companies to discriminate against people with pre-existing conditions, drive up costs for older Americans, and slash Medicaid funding - all while lining the pockets of Big Pharma and other corporate special interests.
My grandmother would be disappointed.
We should be working together to improve the Affordable Care Act. We need to be consulting with constituents, talking to doctors, and hearing from experts, not drafting secret legislation behind closed doors.
[**Please everyone, this bill could be on Donald Trump 's desk to be signed into law before we know it, so we need to make our voices heard. Take action now to protect access to affordable health care.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde82/2a380d31/1b47d7e2/fe9657/1356227807/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20170616_protectaffcare?source=e20170616_healthsignon_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
With every good wish,
Senator Maggie Hassan

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: A lesson from the Maasai tribe
body:
#
**[Every dad wants better opportunities for their kids. Help us make that possible this Father's Day by contributing to our campaign to rebuild Florida into a state that works for all of us >>](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3319/9e00690/4ae3e06c/24037851/2776046865/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170615-maasi-b")**
Team --

If you're familiar with the Maasai Tribe of East Africa, you may know that they greet each other a little differently than we do in the United States. While we might ask, "How are you doing" or "What's up," they ask "How are the children?"

They strive to answer that the children are doing well. And I think that's what we should be striving for here in Florida, too. All of us want to give our kids better opportunities to grow and thrive in our communities.

I'm the father to three incredible children, Caroline and Jackson, who just turned three years old, and Davis, who is one month old. And I'm grateful that R. Jai and I are able to provide them with opportunities that I never dreamed of having growing up. I'm thankful that when I'm asked how my children are doing, I can honestly say they're doing well.

But that's not always the case in this state. If we were to look ourselves in the mirror today, we would have to admit that, as a whole, the children of Florida are not well. Just recently, the Annie E. Casey Foundation ranked Florida 40th in the nation in terms of overall child well-being.

The children of Florida are not well.

They are not well because more than a quarter of them are growing up in poverty.

They are not well because our state has shifted resources from our public schools to private corporations.

They are not well because too many of their families cannot afford healthcare.

They are not well because too many of them cannot afford to go to college without being saddled with a lifetime of debt.

They are not well because their parents are trapped in an economy that delivers record profits for special interests and massive bonuses for those at the top -- and jobs that have shrinking paychecks and too few benefits.

Many of you have reached out to ask about my goals on this campaign, and for the state of Florida. And this is my answer: My goal is to be able to honestly answer that question - "How are the children?" \- with "They are doing well." \- in every county and every corner of this state.

I want to reinvest in our public schools, and ensure that all students have the opportunity to attend college without acquiring a lifetime of debt. I want to expand access to affordable healthcare across our state. I want to invest in creating jobs that pay a living wage, so that all Floridians can have work with dignity. I want to end the vicious cycles of poverty that plague far too many families in our state. **I want to ensure that our children our well.**

[**If you agree, chip in $3 or whatever you can afford to help us fight for Florida 's children in the Governor's office >>**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3319/9e00690/4ae3e06c/24037851/2776046865/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170615-maasi-b")

For too long, our government has focused on increasing profit margins for a few big corporations, instead of increasing opportunities for all of our children. But with your help, we're going to change that. With your help, we will ensure that our children are doing well.

That's what I want for Father's Day, and that's what I want for Florida. Thank you for being a part of this movement.

Bring it home,

Andrew
**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3319/9e00690/4ae3e06c/24037851/2776046865/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170615-maasi-b")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3319/9e00690/4ae3e06c/24037850/2776046865/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3319/9e00690/4ae3e06c/24037853/2776046865/VEsD/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/"). [You can support our campaign by making a donation.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3319/9e00690/4ae3e06c/24037852/2776046865/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-footer")

name: Team Kaine
subject: BREAKING: Corey Stewart looking at challenging Kaine
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
"Republican Corey Stewart is eyeing Senate run against Tim Kaine in 2018"
\- _The Washington Post_ Derek,

Corey Stewart, the candidate who nearly captured the Republican nomination for governor with an anti-immigrant, pro-Confederate platform on Tuesday night, said he's looking to challenge Tim Kaine in the upcoming Senate race.

Just like the president, who mobilized a group of voters that rarely participated in the political process previously, Stewart massively outperformed expectations by courting alt-right activists. This same strategy could have a real impact in a 2018 midterm election where turnout will be volatile -- and we can't afford to rest on our laurels.

If Stewart gets the nomination he'll also have the benefit of the same outside groups that spent more money against Tim Kaine in 2012 than any other Senate candidate in the country.

To beat back this kind of challenge we need a grassroots, people-powered campaign to spread Tim's message of economic opportunity across Virginia -- and that's where you come in.

**[Will you make a contribution and stand with Tim today?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=yzPpOtXlZysZGC%2BBbX%2Bt8paqVr0ThUsA)**

Tim Kaine is fighting tooth and nail against the Trump agenda in the Senate -- and Stewart embodies everything that he is fighting against.

This guy was Donald Trump's campaign chair in Virginia and claimed "I was Trump before Trump."

_The Washington Post_ called him a "xenophobe with a disdain for facts" -- and that was before he compared the effort to remove Confederate monuments in Charlottesville to ISIS.

Seriously. He did that. And still nearly won on Tuesday.

name: Kyle Machado, Director of Organizing
subject: You are Our Revolution's leaders
body:
Working as one united movement
#
Friends,
It was great seeing many of you last weekend at the People's Summit in Chicago. Still more of you tuned in to watch Bernie's speech from home, or got out the vote for Tom Perriello and our progressive slate of candidates running in the Virginia primary. Every one of you made a difference, and it is when we come together that we are strong.
Working as one united movement is precisely what we must do going forward. Our Revolution has four main planks that contribute to our work:
* We unify to organize and build local power
* Cultivate, nominate, and support electoral candidates and ballot initiatives
* Work collectively around national issue campaigns
* Work inside and outside political parties to build power

In order to put our progressive values into practice, each plank of this work is vital. There are phenomenal candidates running for school board, city council, and county commissioner, but it's only through a grassroots infrastructure that we can effectively identify, support, and advocate on their behalf. The same is true for ballot initiatives--groups that can organize, collect petitions, and pass them with voter support hinge on the work we do together. Each local group is autonomous in how they choose to engage in the above issues, but most importantly: we are all growing this movement together.
We're fighting for national issues like Medicare for All, comprehensive immigration reform, and a $15 minimum wage. And while only a few years ago people would have called us crazy for trying, these causes all now enjoy the support of the majority of the American people.
There are hundreds of Our Revolution local groups active across the states, in Puerto Rico, and internationally. We're working towards our common goal of representation for the many, not the few. And though we've only been active in an official capacity for a few months, the hard work that you've put in laid the foundation for the victories that we've achieved together.
[**Please visit our interactive map to find a local group near you, or start one of your own.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0d85/43f17890/ce651d25/620362a3/88408965/VEsH/ "https://ourrevolution.com/groups/?source=em170616-subc")
We're looking forward to what the future holds.
In solidarity,
Kyle Machado
Organizing Director
Our Revolution

**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, June 16th |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,
This past Thursday, I had the opportunity to talk with the Seacoast Republican Women’s (SRW) group. With a packed room of women (and yes, a few men!) it was a great time to share the NHRSC’s plans to continue building our majorities.
They wanted to know what the State Party Chair’s responsibilities entailed. The primary duties include recruitment and fundraising for the Republican Party in NH and representing NH at a national level as a member of the Republican National Committee.
Every day is different, and for the most part inspiring. This past Thursday, I started the day responding to emails and then, on the way to the Seacoast, returning phone calls. After the meeting with the SRW, I traveled back to Concord to meet with Steve Whalley, a long-time local businessman who signed up to run for the Laconia/Belmont seat that has been vacated.
As the Party Chair, I am also in constant contact with House and Senate leadership, staff, and the Governor’s office. I’m pleased to say that the relationship is strong and we are working together to deliver positive results for the taxpayers of New Hampshire (e.g., a good, conservative budget that meets the principles of our Party Platform).
I finished the day with more meetings and talking with folks in Washington about plans to support us going into the 2018 elections.
Folks, as I expected, Republicans are leading the way to a more prosperous New Hampshire with Governor Sununu and our majorities in the House and Senate. Next week both houses will vote on a budget that is fiscally conservative, meets the needs of our citizens, and conforms to our Party Platform—I look forward to the Governor signing it into law in short order.
Sincerely, _Jeanie_

| **House Leaders Applaud Committee of Conference Agreements on Budget**
CONCORD - House Speaker Shawn Jasper (R-Hudson) and House Majority Leader Dick Hinch (R-Merrimack) issued statements following conclusion of the committee of conference negotiations on the final state budget proposal.
**House Majority Leader Dick Hinch (R-Merrimack):**
“Voters sent Republican majorities back to Concord to continue managing our state in a fiscally responsible manner. This budget, like our current budget, meets the needs of our state, and addresses our priorities. We achieve these goals while continuing to make New Hampshire more competitive by reducing the tax burden on our business community, and reducing electricity bills by repealing a tax on electricity consumption. We’ve already received a high level of positive feedback from House Republicans, and I look forward to working with my colleagues in the House to pass this budget next week.”
**House Speaker Shawn Jasper (R-Hudson):**
“This is a budget the legislature and the people of New Hampshire can be proud of. This budget provides resources to address the opioid crisis, mental illness, and domestic violence, includes several reforms to state government, and keeping spending in check. We’ve achieved a balance that ensures our citizens will have access to services they need while reducing the tax burden. Members of the House and Senate have worked tirelessly on this proposal which in my opinion makes sense for our state, our communities, and our constituents.”
_Background: Both Jasper and Hinch serve as members of the committee of conference on HB144 and HB517, the bills that contain the state budget and related policy language. The committee of conference report will be drafted and signed by conferees tomorrow. It will then come before the State Senate next Thursday, June 22. Upon receiving a majority vote from the Senate, the report will need to be approved by a majority the House of Representatives in order to move forward to the Governor’s desk for signature._ |
---
---
---
| _**Senate Republicans**_
**Senators applauds support for state budget approved in Committee of Conference** Concord, NH - Today, the House and Senate agreed to the committee of conference report on HB 144 and HB 517, the bills which make up the Fiscal Year 2018-19 state budget. “The budget agreed upon by the House and Senate works to live within our means and does not include any new taxes or fees while accomplishing a number of critical priorities that will better serve the citizens and businesses of our state,” said Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem). “This budget works to solve problems in our state. In the middle of a heroin crisis it is critical that we double the funding in this budget that goes to the Alcohol Fund. Additional funding will support the programs related to prevention, treatment, recovery and drug interdiction that we hope will curb the crisis,” said Morse. “This budget also allows for the creation of an alcohol and substance abuse treatment wing at the Sununu Center for Youth Services using the remaining funding from FY 17. We realize that we can do a better job preventing this crisis for the next generation by treating addiction early on for our state’s young people.” “Mental health has been a growing concern for our state and this budget provides the funding necessary to expand community services and provide additional beds for the many patients currently waiting for care in hospitals across the state. This budget also works to provide much needed certainty in the availability of community resources for families with a disabled child,” added Morse. “Reducing New Hampshire’s Business Enterprise Tax and Business Profits Tax was a critical priority so that our state will remain a competitive place for small businesses,” said Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels. “We’ve seen positive results from the business tax reductions put into place two years ago, restoring confidence in business, creating good jobs and driving the economy in this state. “The Department of Resources and Economic Development has also been reorganized to better support and promote business and opportunities in this state.” “This budget also eliminates the Electric Consumption Tax, helping our state move in the right direction to reduce the burdensome electric rates paid for by homeowners and businesses. By making changes that will result in lower monthly bills, we put money back in our employer’s pockets and create an improved, lower cost state to run a business,” added Daniels. “We raised the Rainy Day Fund to $100 million in an effort to strengthen our economy and ensure a strong bond rating. It’s the combination of lowering business taxes, saving for a rainy day and reducing the cost of energy that will continue to improve the business climate in New Hampshire.”

* * *

**Senate praises signing expanded mental health beds, DCYF oversight bill into law**
Concord, NH - Today, Governor Chris Sununu signed HB 400 into law, expanding mental health care services and restructuring the Division of Children Youth and Families.
“Seeing first hand all of the people waiting in the hallways at a local hospital for a mental health care bed at New Hampshire Hospital, sometimes for weeks, signaled to us that we needed to take immediate action and address this issue,” said Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem). “This legislation which became law today will help the state of New Hampshire expand the mental health care resources available and develop a 10-year plan for providing care across the state. This legislation reflects our commitment to addressing the mental health care needs of our citizens from all sides now and into the future by increasing direct access to care.”
“I am pleased to see the state come together so quickly on this critical legislation to expand provisions aimed at treating various mental health care needs. It is unacceptable for our citizens to be kept waiting for treatment and this is a positive step for creating a substantive long term plan to alleviating the dramatic need for more mental health care community services in our state,” said Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro). “This legislation also serves to address the need for greater transparency and oversight at the Division of Children Youth and Families to ensure that this agency is protecting New Hampshire’s children from abuse and neglect.”
“Serving on the Commission on Child Abuse Fatalities, it was clear that we needed to do more to protect children in unsafe homes in New Hampshire,” said Senator Sharon Carson (R-Londonderry). “Realizing there are a number of issues that need to be addressed, expanding the agency oversight by creating the office of the child advocate is one way we could make significant progress. This leadership position will be a direct line from the governor’s office and will provide greater transparency within the agency. We hope this will ultimately allow us to make stronger recommendations for the improved safety and future of our kids.”
“I am pleased by the Governor’s support of this measure by signing this bill into law today and hope we can continue to make prudent steps toward ensuring the safety of every one of our state’s children.”

clean_body: | | | NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, June 16th | ---|---|--- | | A Note From Jeanie | | | Dear Derek, This past Thursday, I had the opportunity to talk with the Seacoast Republican Women’s (SRW) group. With a packed room of women (and yes, a few men!) it was a great time to share the NHRSC’s plans to continue building our majorities. They wanted to know what the State Party Chair’s responsibilities entailed. The primary duties include recruitment and fundraising for the Republican Party in NH and representing NH at a national level as a member of the Republican National Committee. Every day is different, and for the most part inspiring. This past Thursday, I started the day responding to emails and then, on the way to the Seacoast, returning phone calls. After the meeting with the SRW, I traveled back to Concord to meet with Steve Whalley, a long-time local businessman who signed up to run for the Laconia/Belmont seat that has been vacated. As the Party Chair, I am also in constant contact with House and Senate leadership, staff, and the Governor’s office. I’m pleased to say that the relationship is strong and we are working together to deliver positive results for the taxpayers of New Hampshire (e.g., a good, conservative budget that meets the principles of our Party Platform). I finished the day with more meetings and talking with folks in Washington about plans to support us going into the 2018 elections. Folks, as I expected, Republicans are leading the way to a more prosperous New Hampshire with Governor Sununu and our majorities in the House and Senate. Next week both houses will vote on a budget that is fiscally conservative, meets the needs of our citizens, and conforms to our Party Platform—I look forward to the Governor signing it into law in short order. Sincerely, Jeanie | | | Join the Grassroots Circle! | | The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. | | | | Intern with the NH GOP! | | Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics? We will pick the lucky winner on Monday! | ---|---|--- | Photo of the Week | ---|---|--- Wednesday, the NHGOP hosted a Birthday Party for President Donald J. Trump at Murphy's Taproom in Manchester. | Republican Spotlight! | ---|---|--- | Thank you for all of your submissions! This week's spotlight shines on Mary Donnelly! Mary was an early supporter of President Donald Trump and attended the Republican National Convention in Cleveland to show her support. Mary works very had to support President Trump in the state of New Hampshire and even helped plan his 71st Birthday Party at Murphy's Taproom. We thank Mary for all of her hard work and dedication to the Republican Party! Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? | | | Legislative Highlights | ---|---|--- | House Republicans | | House Leaders Applaud Committee of Conference Agreements on Budget CONCORD - House Speaker Shawn Jasper (R-Hudson) and House Majority Leader Dick Hinch (R-Merrimack) issued statements following conclusion of the committee of conference negotiations on the final state budget proposal. House Majority Leader Dick Hinch (R-Merrimack): “Voters sent Republican majorities back to Concord to continue managing our state in a fiscally responsible manner. This budget, like our current budget, meets the needs of our state, and addresses our priorities. We achieve these goals while continuing to make New Hampshire more competitive by reducing the tax burden on our business community, and reducing electricity bills by repealing a tax on electricity consumption. We’ve already received a high level of positive feedback from House Republicans, and I look forward to working with my colleagues in the House to pass this budget next week.” House Speaker Shawn Jasper (R-Hudson): “This is a budget the legislature and the people of New Hampshire can be proud of. This budget provides resources to address the opioid crisis, mental illness, and domestic violence, includes several reforms to state government, and keeping spending in check. We’ve achieved a balance that ensures our citizens will have access to services they need while reducing the tax burden. Members of the House and Senate have worked tirelessly on this proposal which in my opinion makes sense for our state, our communities, and our constituents.” Background: Both Jasper and Hinch serve as members of the committee of conference on HB144 and HB517, the bills that contain the state budget and related policy language. The committee of conference report will be drafted and signed by conferees tomorrow. It will then come before the State Senate next Thursday, June 22. Upon receiving a majority vote from the Senate, the report will need to be approved by a majority the House of Representatives in order to move forward to the Governor’s desk for signature. | | Senate Republicans Senators applauds support for state budget approved in Committee of Conference Concord, NH - Today, the House and Senate agreed to the committee of conference report on HB 144 and HB 517, the bills which make up the Fiscal Year 2018-19 state budget. “The budget agreed upon by the House and Senate works to live within our means and does not include any new taxes or fees while accomplishing a number of critical priorities that will better serve the citizens and businesses of our state,” said Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem). “This budget works to solve problems in our state. In the middle of a heroin crisis it is critical that we double the funding in this budget that goes to the Alcohol Fund. Additional funding will support the programs related to prevention, treatment, recovery and drug interdiction that we hope will curb the crisis,” said Morse. “This budget also allows for the creation of an alcohol and substance abuse treatment wing at the Sununu Center for Youth Services using the remaining funding from FY 17. We realize that we can do a better job preventing this crisis for the next generation by treating addiction early on for our state’s young people.” “Mental health has been a growing concern for our state and this budget provides the funding necessary to expand community services and provide additional beds for the many patients currently waiting for care in hospitals across the state. This budget also works to provide much needed certainty in the availability of community resources for families with a disabled child,” added Morse. “Reducing New Hampshire’s Business Enterprise Tax and Business Profits Tax was a critical priority so that our state will remain a competitive place for small businesses,” said Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels. “We’ve seen positive results from the business tax reductions put into place two years ago, restoring confidence in business, creating good jobs and driving the economy in this state. “The Department of Resources and Economic Development has also been reorganized to better support and promote business and opportunities in this state.” “This budget also eliminates the Electric Consumption Tax, helping our state move in the right direction to reduce the burdensome electric rates paid for by homeowners and businesses. By making changes that will result in lower monthly bills, we put money back in our employer’s pockets and create an improved, lower cost state to run a business,” added Daniels. “We raised the Rainy Day Fund to $100 million in an effort to strengthen our economy and ensure a strong bond rating. It’s the combination of lowering business taxes, saving for a rainy day and reducing the cost of energy that will continue to improve the business climate in New Hampshire.” Senate praises signing expanded mental health beds, DCYF oversight bill into law Concord, NH - Today, Governor Chris Sununu signed HB 400 into law, expanding mental health care services and restructuring the Division of Children Youth and Families. “Seeing first hand all of the people waiting in the hallways at a local hospital for a mental health care bed at New Hampshire Hospital, sometimes for weeks, signaled to us that we needed to take immediate action and address this issue,” said Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem). “This legislation which became law today will help the state of New Hampshire expand the mental health care resources available and develop a 10-year plan for providing care across the state. This legislation reflects our commitment to addressing the mental health care needs of our citizens from all sides now and into the future by increasing direct access to care.” “I am pleased to see the state come together so quickly on this critical legislation to expand provisions aimed at treating various mental health care needs. It is unacceptable for our citizens to be kept waiting for treatment and this is a positive step for creating a substantive long term plan to alleviating the dramatic need for more mental health care community services in our state,” said Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro). “This legislation also serves to address the need for greater transparency and oversight at the Division of Children Youth and Families to ensure that this agency is protecting New Hampshire’s children from abuse and neglect.” “Serving on the Commission on Child Abuse Fatalities, it was clear that we needed to do more to protect children in unsafe homes in New Hampshire,” said Senator Sharon Carson (R-Londonderry). “Realizing there are a number of issues that need to be addressed, expanding the agency oversight by creating the office of the child advocate is one way we could make significant progress. This leadership position will be a direct line from the governor’s office and will provide greater transparency within the agency. We hope this will ultimately allow us to make stronger recommendations for the improved safety and future of our kids.” “I am pleased by the Governor’s support of this measure by signing this bill into law today and hope we can continue to make prudent steps toward ensuring the safety of every one of our state’s children.” | | Meet Sen. David Boutin | ---|---|--- | | | Senator David Boutin is the Republican candidate for the special election in Senate District 16, which covers Hooksett, Candia, Bow, Dunbarton, and Manchester wards 1, 2, 12. Peter,

This month, Secretary DeVos was back in the Senate, defending **President Trump 's plan to cut $9 billion in public education funding -- money that goes toward college affordability, student loan forgiveness, workforce development, and our children's education.**

Protecting Hoosier families from slash and burn cuts like the Trump/Devos budget is exactly why Joe ran for Senate six years ago. And that's exactly why special interest groups and GOP megadonors are rallying to defeat Joe in 2018 -- to trade Joe's hard work and independence for another Republican rubber stamp for the Trump agenda.

With Donald Trump in the White House and Betsy DeVos in the administration, it's absolutely critical that we keep Joe in the Senate fighting to protect public education. **And to do that, we have to hit our mid-month, $20,000 fundraising goal.** **[Can you pitch in $5 to make sure we don't fall short?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2600/7377f838/3660367d/51fb66ad/2899815946/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-mmjune?refcode=20170616nd&amount=10")**

As a former school board president, Joe knows the value of public education and won't let DeVos chip away at it. He's working **to expand access to pre-K for Indiana 's children, make college more affordable for our students, and improve workforce development programs.**

For years, Secretary DeVos has dumped millions into candidates and organizations with the intention of bankrupting Indiana's public schools at the expense of our children. With Joe in the Senate, Hoosiers know they have someone who will always fight for them, not multimillion dollar donors.

**[Will you chip in $5 now to help us hit our mid-month goal and send Joe back to the Senate to fight for public education?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2600/7377f838/3660367d/51fb66ad/2899815946/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-mmjune?refcode=20170616nd&amount=10")**

Joe will always stand up for our children. Let's show Joe we stand with him.

\-- Team Donnelly
